justifications.completeness: Captures the single pedestrian and scene context but only 2 of 7 ground-truth vehicles, missing most vehicle instances.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes (action, context, age, motion and door/trunk states) for detected entities, offering rich semantics for those few detections.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes several spatial relations but the pedestrian relation contradicts the ground-truth simple context and many objects lack spatial relations, reducing accuracy.
justifications.overall: Accurate and semantically rich for the detected pedestrian and listed vehicles but poor object recall and some incorrect spatial assertions result in moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of two pedestrians and one of two vehicles, missing the bending/closing person and the second vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes (actions, context, age, vehicle door/trunk states) and an explicit scene description, though it omits attributes for the missed objects.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives reasonable spatial relations (parked on the right, near the pedestrian) that are consistent and useful, but lacks relations for the missing person and vehicle.
justifications.overall: Accurate for detected objects with good attribute detail and spatial relations, but overall performance is limited by missed objects leading to moderate completeness.

justifications.completeness: Most actors and core attributes are present but the second pedestrian's simple context 'closing' was missed and vehicle trunk and door states were asserted without ground truth support.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes many semantic labels beyond the ground truth, such as spatial relations and object attributes, but it also adds unsupported details and omits one contextual label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Spatial relations are provided for all objects but the first pedestrian is placed in the center of the road while the ground truth implies walking along the side, indicating at least one significant spatial error.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and rich annotations overall, but mistakes in contextual labeling and spatial placement reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: Both pedestrians are detected and largely annotated but the system missed the parked vehicle and incorrectly labeled the bending person as transporting, so some ground truth elements are missing or wrong.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides many attributes (actions, age, contextual notes, transporting) adding detail, but it contains an incorrect transporting label and omits the vehicle semantics from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are specific and consistent with the scene description (left person near cart, right person on the right and walking away) and appear accurate.
justifications.overall: The VLM produces a useful and mostly accurate pedestrian-centric description with strong spatial detail, but errors in an action-related attribute and the missing vehicle lower the overall quality.
